useCases: ICP/IEPE accelerometers (PCB Piezotronics, Brüel & Kjær), vibration monitoring, modal analysis, predictive maintenance
notes: IEPE/ICP sensors use constant-current excitation (typically 4mA) with AC-coupled signal superimposed on DC bias. 10-32 UNF Microdot is the miniature threaded connector on small accelerometers. BNC adapter pigtails common.

name: LVDT/RVDT Connector
useCases: Linear position sensing (LVDT), rotary position sensing (RVDT), hydraulic cylinder stroke, aircraft flight control actuator feedback
locking: MS circular (mil-spec sensors), M12 or D-sub (industrial)
notes: LVDT/RVDT are analog sensors — output is AC voltage proportional to displacement. Signal conditioning (demodulation) is external. No standard connector — mil uses MS circular, industrial uses M12 or D-sub.

name: Eddy Current Probe Connector (BNC/Binder)
useCases: Turbine shaft vibration (API 670), bearing clearance, precision gap measurement, semiconductor wafer flatness
notes: Eddy current probes typically have integral cable (non-removable) that terminates in BNC or proprietary connector to the driver/proximitor. API 670 specifies installation requirements for turbomachinery protection.

name: Strain Gauge / Wheatstone Bridge Terminal
useCases: Structural health monitoring, bridge load testing, pressure transducers (internal), torque sensors, aerospace structural testing
notes: Similar to load cell — Wheatstone bridge measurement. DAQ systems (HBM/HBK, NI) use D-sub 9 or 15 pin for bridge inputs. Strain gauges themselves are soldered/bonded — connector is at the cable-to-instrument interface.

name: Geophone Connector (military/Bendix)
useCases: Seismic exploration (oil & gas), earthquake monitoring, geotechnical monitoring, military ground surveillance (UGS)
notes: Geophones produce millivolt-level analog signal from ground vibration. Seismic acquisition systems (Sercel, INOVA) use proprietary connectors for geophone strings. Military UGS use mil-spec circular.

name: pH/ORP Probe Connector (BNC)
useCases: pH meters, ORP measurement, water treatment, pharmaceutical, laboratory analysis, swimming pool automation
notes: Uses BNC connector but the cable is high-impedance (triaxial or high-insulation coax). Standard 50Ω BNC cables will cause measurement drift. Low-noise triax cable required. VP (Vario Pin) connector is alternative in some Endress+Hauser probes.

name: Flow Meter Pulse Output (DIN 43650 / M12)
useCases: Electromagnetic flow meters, vortex flow meters, turbine flow meters, paddle wheel sensors
notes: DIN 43650 Form A (18x18mm industry standard) used on solenoid valves and flow meter outputs. Form B (smaller) and Form C (smallest). Being replaced by M12 A-coded in new designs.

name: DIN 43650 Form A (Solenoid Valve Connector)
useCases: Solenoid valves (pneumatic, hydraulic), proportional valves, industrial actuators, pneumatic valve islands
notes: THE universal solenoid valve connector. 18mm x 18mm square form. Central screw clamps connector to valve solenoid pins. LED/varistor/diode suppression circuits available integrated into connector body.

name: Pressure Transducer Connector (Packard Metripack / DIN 43650 / M12)
useCases: Hydraulic pressure sensors, pneumatic pressure transmitters, automotive oil/fuel pressure, HVAC pressure
locking: Depends on sensor type (Metripack: automotive / DIN 43650: industrial / M12: modern industrial)
notes: Automotive pressure sensors often use Packard Metripack or Deutsch. Industrial uses DIN 43650 or M12. Output: 0.5–4.5V ratiometric (automotive) or 4-20mA (industrial).

name: LEMO B-Series (Plastic, Medical)
useCases: Patient monitoring cables (SpO2, ECG lead wires), infusion pumps, surgical instruments, endoscopy, dialysis machines
notes: Plastic body for electrical isolation (patient safety). Color-coded shells for function identification (SpO2 = blue, ECG = gray, etc.). Sterilizable variants for surgical use. Self-latching prevents accidental disconnection during procedures.

name: LEMO T-Series (Elbow / Low-Profile)
useCases: Wearable patient monitors, ambulatory ECG (Holter), compact surgical tools, body-worn sensors
notes: Right-angle variant of LEMO push-pull for low-profile body-worn applications. Reduces cable strain and snag risk on patient.

name: Redel SP (LEMO Plastic Circular)
useCases: Reusable surgical instruments, diagnostic equipment, dental handpieces, ultrasound probes
notes: Designed for repeated sterilization (autoclave at 134°C). Multiple keying options prevent cross-connection between different instrument types. High mating cycle life (5000+).

name: ODU MEDI-SNAP
useCases: Patient cables, infusion pumps, respiratory devices, powered surgical instruments, defibrillator paddles
notes: Breakaway feature disconnects cleanly under excessive pull — prevents patient injury or device damage if cable is snagged. Sterilizable. Color-coded.

name: Fischer MiniMax (High-Density Medical)
useCases: Portable ultrasound, endoscope cameras, surgical robotics data links, soldier-worn medical
notes: Extremely high pin density for size — 75 contacts in 12mm shell. Mixed signal+power+coax+fluid in single connector possible. Used in da Vinci surgical robot cabling.

name: Omnetics Nano Circular / Nano Strip
useCases: Neural recording arrays, cochlear implant external interfaces, brain-computer interfaces, in-vivo neuroscience probes
notes: Ultra-miniature for implantable and neuroscience applications. Biocompatible plating options. Used with Utah arrays and other neural electrode arrays. Omnetics is the dominant supplier for neuroscience connectors.

name: Hypertronics (Hermetic Medical)
useCases: Pacemaker feedthroughs, implantable neurostimulators, cochlear implant internal, implantable drug pumps
notes: Hermetic feedthrough connectors for implantable medical devices. Must maintain hermeticity (leak rate <1×10⁻⁸ atm·cc/sec He) for device lifetime (10+ years). Biocompatible materials (titanium, platinum, alumina ceramic).

name: ECG Snap Connector (4mm snap stud)
useCases: ECG electrode connection, Holter monitor leads, stress test leads, neonatal monitoring
notes: 4mm press stud mates with disposable Ag/AgCl gel electrode. Lead wires terminated with snap connector on one end, device-specific connector (LEMO, proprietary) on other end. Color-coded by lead placement (AHA vs IEC color scheme).

name: ECG Banana/Pin Connector (DIN 42802)
useCases: European ECG machines, reusable ECG electrodes (limb clamps, suction cups)
notes: 1.5mm safety banana pin — smaller than standard 4mm lab banana. European/Indian standard for reusable ECG electrodes. Cannot be inserted into standard 4mm lab banana sockets — patient safety design.

name: SpO2 Sensor Connector (Nellcor/Masimo/Philips)
useCases: Pulse oximeter finger/ear/forehead sensors, patient monitors (SpO2 channel)
notes: Every SpO2 manufacturer uses a proprietary connector — Nellcor OxiMax (DB-9 based), Masimo LNCS (proprietary), Philips (8-pin), GE (TruSignal). NOT interchangeable. Connector encodes sensor type and calibration data.

name: Luer Lock / Luer Slip (Electrical Hybrid)
useCases: Powered injection systems, smart IV sets, electronically monitored catheters
notes: Hybrid connectors combining Luer fluid path with electrical contacts for smart medical devices. ISO 80369 series defines different Luer variants to prevent misconnections between applications (enteral/IV/respiratory).

name: Medical Gas Connector (DISS / NIST)
useCases: Anesthesia machines, ventilator gas supply, medical gas pipeline outlets, oxygen concentrators
voltage: N/A (gas connector with electrical interlock on some)
notes: DISS (Diameter Index Safety System) uses unique thread diameter per gas to prevent cross-connection. NIST (Non-Interchangeable Screw Thread) similar concept for British/Commonwealth standard. Indian hospitals use a mix of both.

name: SubConn Micro Circular
useCases: ROV sensors, underwater cameras, oceanographic instruments, AUV peripherals, marine biology sensors
notes: Wet-mateable to 10,000m depth. Molded polyurethane body with stainless or titanium shell. Standard in scientific and commercial ROV industry. Oil-filled contacts for reliable underwater mating.

name: SubConn Power Series
useCases: ROV power distribution, subsea pump power, underwater vehicle traction, subsea BOP control
notes: High-power subsea connector for ROV umbilicals and subsea production systems. Pressure-rated to 10,000m.

name: SubConn Ethernet / Fiber
useCases: Underwater HD video, subsea Ethernet telemetry, ROV control data, AUV communication, ocean observatory networks
notes: Copper Ethernet (100/1000BASE-T) and fiber optic variants for high-bandwidth subsea data. Wet-mateable fiber optic uses expanded beam technology to tolerate seawater between fiber faces during mating.

name: Impulse MCIL/MCBH (MIL-spec Subsea)
useCases: Ocean bottom seismometers, deep-sea moorings, CTD instruments, tsunami sensors (DART buoys)
notes: MCIL = in-line cable connector, MCBH = bulkhead. Titanium and PEEK housings for full ocean depth (11,000m). Standard on WHOI (Woods Hole) and MBARI instruments.

name: Burton Subsea Connector
useCases: Offshore oil platform control systems, subsea Christmas tree controls, umbilical terminations, subsea valve actuators
notes: High-reliability connectors for subsea oil & gas production. Rated for 25+ year subsea deployment. ROV-installable variants for deepwater fields. Combined electrical + hydraulic + optical connectors available.

name: Wet-Mate Connector (Siemens/OneSubsea, Teledyne)
useCases: Subsea production system connections, ROV hot-stab operations, subsea processing, subsea power grid nodes
notes: Designed to be connected and disconnected underwater by ROV, without dry environment. Critical for subsea field reconfiguration. Siemens/OneSubsea DigiTAL and Teledyne ODI are major suppliers.

name: Marine Cable Gland (IP68)
useCases: Cable entry into junction boxes on ships, offshore platforms, marine engines, deck equipment
notes: Not a signal connector but critical for marine/offshore cable management. Brass, stainless, or polyamide body. Double-compression for enhanced sealing. EMC versions with braided shield contact.

name: Amphenol Ecomate Aquarius
useCases: Deck-mounted marine electronics, outdoor cameras, solar farm junction boxes, pool/fountain lighting
notes: Ruggedized circular with IP68/IP69K sealing. Thermoplastic body — corrosion-free for marine environments. Used as cost-effective alternative to full subsea connectors in shallow/splash zone applications.

name: Space-Grade D-sub (MIL-DTL-24308, Class K/S)
useCases: Satellite bus wiring, CubeSat PCB interconnects, launch vehicle avionics, space instrument harnesses
standard: MIL-DTL-24308 (space screening per Class K/S), ECSS-Q-ST-70
notes: Standard D-sub with space-grade screening: outgassing per ASTM E595, radiation testing, thermal vacuum qualification, lot acceptance testing. ESA ECSS and NASA EEE-INST-002 specify screening levels.

name: Micro-D Space-Grade (MIL-DTL-83513, Class K/S)
useCases: Satellite subsystem interconnects, star tracker, reaction wheel harness, solar array drive, CubeSat high-density I/O
notes: Most common space connector family. Mass-critical — Micro-D saves ~60% mass vs standard D-sub. Outgassing-qualified materials. Pin counts up to 100 in small shell.

name: Nano-D Space-Grade (MIL-DTL-32139, Class K/S)
useCases: CubeSat/SmallSat PCB interconnects, nano-satellite harnesses, space-grade FPGAs, star sensor boards
notes: Smallest standard space connector. Critical for CubeSat where volume/mass is extremely constrained. 0.635mm pitch. Limited current capacity — signal-only in most applications.

name: Hermetic Feedthrough (Glass-to-Metal Seal)
useCases: Vacuum chamber instrumentation, satellite pressure vessel penetrations, cryostat wiring, particle accelerator detectors, fusion reactor diagnostics
notes: Glass-to-metal or ceramic-to-metal seal maintains vacuum/pressure boundary. ConFlat (CF) or KF flange mounting for vacuum systems. Critical spec: helium leak rate. Used in CERN detectors, ITER diagnostics, ISRO satellite instruments.

name: Hermetic Feedthrough (Ceramic-to-Metal)
useCases: High-temperature vacuum furnaces, semiconductor process chambers, ion implanters, space thruster (ion engine) power
notes: Ceramic (Al₂O₃) provides better HV insulation and higher temp than glass seal. Used in semiconductor fab equipment (etch chambers, CVD), space ion thrusters (ISRO GSLV, NASA NEXT), and fusion research.

name: SMA Hermetic (Vacuum RF Feedthrough)
useCases: Vacuum chamber RF feed, plasma processing, particle accelerator RF, space instrument RF, satellite antenna feed
notes: Standard SMA interface with hermetic glass/ceramic seal on a KF or CF vacuum flange. Maintains 50Ω impedance through vacuum wall. Used in every vacuum-based RF application from semiconductor to space.

name: Glenair Mighty Mouse (Series 801/805/807)
useCases: Satellite harnesses, space instrument connectors, missile systems, UAV avionics, helmet-mounted displays
standard: Based on MIL-DTL-38999 insert pattern in smaller shells
notes: Glenair proprietary miniaturized circular connectors. Smaller shells than equivalent 38999 for same pin count. Lightweight composite shells available for mass-critical space applications. Used on ISRO and NASA spacecraft.

name: Space-Grade Fiber Optic (MIL-PRF-29504, Diamond AVIM)
useCases: Satellite optical data links, space telescope instruments (JWST), inter-satellite laser comm terminals, star tracker fiber coupling
locking: Threaded / bayonet (in 38999 shell) or Diamond AVIM push-pull
notes: Space-qualified fiber termini. Diamond AVIM is the dominant space fiber connector (used on JWST). Radiation-hardened fiber (fluorine-doped or pure silica core) required for space radiation environment.

name: Harwin Datamate / Gecko
useCases: CubeSat PCB stack connectors, small satellite bus boards, space-grade board-to-board, UAV controller PCBs
notes: Harwin connectors are very popular in CubeSat/SmallSat community due to smaller size/mass than Micro-D while having space-qualified variants. Gecko 1.25mm pitch is popular for high-density CubeSat stacks. ISRO and many NewSpace companies use Harwin extensively.

name: Omnetics Latching Nano / Micro Circular
useCases: Satellite instrument harnesses, space-grade sensor connections, military helmet-mounted displays, neural interfaces
notes: Omnetics adds latching mechanism to Micro-D/Nano-D form factor — prevents accidental disconnection under vibration. Popular for space instruments and military avionics where standard screw-lock is too slow for field use.

name: Ex d Flameproof Circular Connector
useCases: Offshore oil platforms (Zone 1), petrochemical plants, refineries, gas processing, coal mines, paint spray booths
locking: Flameproof threaded coupling with certification markings
notes: Flameproof enclosure — internal explosion cannot propagate to surrounding atmosphere. Flame path (thread engagement length + gap) is precisely specified and certified. Cable entry via Ex d cable gland.

name: Ex e Increased Safety Connector
useCases: Chemical plants, grain handling, pharmaceutical dust areas, refineries (non-sparking circuits)
notes: Increased safety — no sparking or hot surfaces under normal operation. Enhanced creepage and clearance distances prevent arc formation. Used for lighting, motors, and junction boxes in hazardous areas.

name: Ex i Intrinsically Safe Connector (Turck/Pepperl+Fuchs)
useCases: IS field instruments in Zone 0 (most hazardous), gas detectors, IS sensor loops, IS 4-20mA transmitters
locking: Standard M12 or proprietary (IS barrier limits energy)
notes: Intrinsically safe — energy in circuit is too low to ignite explosive atmosphere under any fault condition. Ex ia = safe with 2 faults (Zone 0). Ex ib = safe with 1 fault (Zone 1). IS barrier/isolator on safe side limits voltage/current.

name: Ex d Cable Gland (Flameproof)
useCases: Cable entry into Ex d enclosures, junction boxes, motors, control panels in Zone 1/2
notes: Certified flameproof cable gland — maintains Ex d integrity of enclosure. Inner and outer seals. Barrier type for cable with different armor/braid. Thread: M20, M25, M32, M40, M50, M63, M75 standard. Indian PESO (Petroleum & Explosives Safety Organisation) certification required for Indian hazardous installations.

name: ATEX/IECEx M12 Connector (Zone 2/22)
useCases: Sensors and actuators in Zone 2 (gas) or Zone 22 (dust), non-sparking applications in low-risk hazardous areas
notes: Standard M12 connectors certified for Zone 2/22 — Type n (non-sparking). Allows standard industrial sensors in Zone 2 without full Ex d/e treatment. Many IFM, Sick, Balluff sensors available in Ex nA M12 variants.

name: Stahl / R. Stahl IS Interface Connector
useCases: PROFIBUS PA in Zone 1, Foundation Fieldbus in hazardous area, IS segment couplers
notes: R. Stahl and Pepperl+Fuchs make complete IS fieldbus infrastructure. FISCO (Fieldbus Intrinsically Safe COncept) defines entity parameters for IS fieldbus segments. Simplified IS certification for fieldbus.

name: Kemlon HP/HT Connector
useCases: Downhole logging tools, MWD/LWD (Measurement While Drilling), ESP (Electric Submersible Pump) power, reservoir monitoring
notes: Rated for extreme downhole conditions: 260°C, 30,000 psi. Metal-to-metal seal critical at these pressures. Used in ESP motor connections and logging tool strings.

name: Greene Tweed / Seal-Connect
useCases: HP/HT well completions, subsea tree electrical penetrations, wellhead pressure penetrations
locking: Metal-to-metal seal with pressure test port
notes: Combines electrical connection with qualified pressure seal at wellhead/subsea tree. Pressure integrity verified via test port before well activation.

name: Glenair / Teledyne Downhole Circular
useCases: Downhole pressure/temperature gauges, formation evaluation sensors, production logging tools, nuclear logging
notes: Smaller pin counts for sensor signals. Must withstand thermal cycling during tripping in/out of well. Vibration from drilling operations. Often deployed with wireline or coiled tubing.

name: ESP Motor Pothead Connector
useCases: Electric Submersible Pump motor power connection, artificial lift systems, deepwater ESP installations
notes: Critical connection between downhole power cable and ESP motor. Failure here kills the ESP — well must be pulled. Multi-barrier seal design. Round cable to flat motor lead transition.

name: Wet-Connect / Inductive Coupler (Subsea Well)
useCases: Permanent downhole gauges in subsea wells, intelligent well completion, SCSSV control, ICV monitoring
notes: No physical electrical contact — power and data transferred inductively through pressure boundary. Eliminates penetration through pressure barrier. Used in permanent subsea well monitoring installations.

name: Amphenol Nuclear (Series 1/2/3)
useCases: Reactor containment penetrations, safety system wiring, post-accident monitoring, nuclear reactor building I&C
notes: Must survive LOCA (Loss of Coolant Accident): 400°C steam, chemical spray, 200+ Mrad gamma radiation. Qualified per IEEE 323 (Class 1E). Amphenol and TE are primary suppliers. Indian NPCIL (Nuclear Power Corporation) uses these in PHWR and LWR reactors.

name: EPC / Electric Penetration Assembly (Nuclear)
useCases: Electrical penetration through reactor containment wall, safety bus power, instrumentation feedthrough
standard: IEEE 317, ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Section III
notes: The most critical nuclear connector — maintains containment integrity. Must pass LOCA qualification, seismic qualification (IEEE 344), and radiation aging. Helium leak tested. Design life 40–60 years. Indian Kudankulam VVER and upcoming pressurized heavy water reactors use these.

name: Souriau 8500 Series (Radiation-Hardened)
useCases: Nuclear fuel handling equipment, hot cell manipulators, spent fuel pool instrumentation, particle accelerators (CERN)
notes: Materials selected for radiation resistance: PEEK, glass-filled polyimide, ceramic insulators. Rated for 1 GGy (10⁹ Gy) integrated gamma dose. Used in CERN accelerator tunnels and nuclear reprocessing facilities. Standard 38999 insert patterns in radiation-hardened housings.

name: Conax Nuclear Feedthrough
useCases: Thermocouple and RTD feedthroughs into reactor vessels, containment building instrument penetrations, research reactors
standard: ASME B&PV Code Section III, IEEE 317
notes: Conax specializes in multi-element TC/RTD penetrations — single fitting can pass 50+ thermocouple pairs through containment wall. Swagelok-style compression creates seal around individual wires. Used in BARC (Bhabha Atomic Research Centre) research reactors.

name: Mineral-Insulated Cable Termination (MI Cable)
useCases: Nuclear safety circuits, fire alarm circuits (fire-rated), reactor instrumentation, emergency lighting, submarine wiring
notes: Copper-sheathed, MgO-insulated cable — inherently fireproof and radiation-hard. Termination requires pot seal to prevent moisture ingress into hygroscopic MgO insulation. Used for all nuclear safety circuits and Class 1E wiring. Also used in fire-rated building wiring per Indian NBC 2016.

name: Dinse (cross-ref from Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1. MIG/TIG/Stick welding machine connections.
notes: Cross-reference — main entry in Phase 1C.

name: Tweco / Lincoln Twist-Mate
useCases: MIG gun connection to wire feeder/power source, Lincoln and Miller welding platforms
notes: NOT cross-brand compatible — Tweco, Lincoln, Miller each have proprietary MIG gun connectors. Euro-style (cross-ref below) is the universal alternative.

name: Euro-Style MIG Connector (Dinse + gas)
useCases: European/universal MIG gun connection, aftermarket MIG guns, multi-brand compatibility
notes: Combines Dinse power connector with gas and trigger wire connections in a standard format. Allows cross-brand MIG gun compatibility. European standard adopted globally.

name: TIG Torch Connector (Dinse + gas + water)
useCases: TIG torch connection to power source, water-cooled TIG torches, high-amp TIG welding
notes: Water-cooled TIG torches have water supply and return lines in addition to power and gas. Torch adapters available for cross-brand compatibility.

name: Stud Welding Gun Connector
useCases: Drawn-arc stud welding (structural steel, shipbuilding), capacitor discharge stud welding (thin sheet)
notes: Extremely high peak current for very short duration (3–1000ms). Gun connector must handle current pulse without overheating. Nelson and HBS are dominant stud welding equipment suppliers.

name: Spot Welding Electrode Holder / Shank
useCases: Automotive body welding, sheet metal fabrication, battery tab welding, appliance manufacturing
notes: Not a connector in traditional sense — electrodes are held by tapered shank in welding gun arm. RWMA Class 1/2/3 copper alloys. Electrode caps are consumable. Automotive body shops consume thousands of electrode caps per day.

name: Stäubli CombiTac
useCases: Robot tool changer multi-connect, test bench quick-disconnect, production line modular coupling, automotive assembly fixtures
pins: Custom configuration: electrical + pneumatic + hydraulic + data + fiber in one frame
locking: Lever-operated frame lock (single motion connects all media)
notes: Modular frame accepts any combination of electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, thermocouple, data, and fiber optic modules. Single lever connects everything simultaneously. Used on industrial robot tool changers (e.g., ABB, KUKA) for multi-media quick-change.

name: ATI Industrial Automation Tool Changer
useCases: Industrial robot end-effector quick-change, welding robot gun change, CNC machine tool change, assembly robot gripper swap
notes: Master on robot wrist, Tool on end-effector. Pneumatic lock engages automatically — robot drops tool if air pressure is lost (fail-safe). Electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, and signal modules snap into tool changer frame. Standard in automotive and general industrial robotics.

name: Harting Han-Modular
useCases: Machine tool connections (power + signal + data + pneumatic), industrial robot dress packs, textile/packaging machine interfaces
pins: Modular: mix of power, signal, data (RJ45, USB, fiber), pneumatic in one housing
notes: Han-Modular system: standard outer housing accepts interchangeable inner modules. Hundreds of module combinations. Common on European machine tools — single connector carries machine power, control, Ethernet, and pneumatic. Also used for modular container data centers.

name: Harting Han B/E/D (Standard Industrial Rectangular)
useCases: Motor connections, crane power, conveyor drives, machine power feeds, HVAC chiller connections, Indian industrial panels
pins: Han B: 6–24 / Han E: 6–48 / Han D: 9–72
notes: Classic industrial rectangular connector. Han B = standard, Han E = compact, Han D = high-density. Surface-mount and panel-mount housings. Standard for European machine building. Indian industrial panels commonly use Han B for motor connections.

name: ILME / Sibas / Weidmüller HDC (Han-compatible)
useCases: Same applications as Harting Han — motor connections, machine interfaces, panel building
notes: ILME, Sibas (TE), and Weidmüller HDC are interchangeable with Harting Han — same insert patterns and housing dimensions per IEC 61984. Provides second-source options for panel builders.

name: Multi-Coupling Plate (Hydraulic + Electrical)
useCases: Excavator attachments (quick hitch), agricultural implements (tractor 3-point), forestry equipment, mobile crane outrigger
notes: Combines multiple flat-face hydraulic quick couplings with electrical connector in single docking plate. One lever connects all hydraulic and electrical lines simultaneously when attaching implement to machine. Critical for modern excavator quick-hitch systems.

name: ISOBUS (ISO 11783) Connector
useCases: Tractor-to-implement communication, precision agriculture, GPS-guided implements, variable rate application
notes: Standard for tractor-implement communication. Carries CAN bus (250kbps) + power. Allows any ISOBUS-compliant implement to work with any ISOBUS tractor regardless of brand. Based on M12 9-pin variant. AEF (Agricultural Industry Electronics Foundation) manages certification.

name: 2.54mm Pin Header (0.1" Generic)
useCases: Arduino shields, breadboard jumper wires, Raspberry Pi GPIO, prototyping, hobbyist electronics, 3D printer boards (Marlin/Klipper)
notes: THE universal prototyping header. Through-hole. Gold or tin plated. Single-row and dual-row. Mates with Dupont/BLS crimp housings, jumper wires, or female header sockets. Samtec, Amphenol, Würth, and dozens of generics.

name: Dupont / BLS 2.54mm Crimp Housing
useCases: Jumper wires for Arduino/RPi, 3D printer wiring (endstops, fans, thermistors), hobbyist harnesses, breadboard hookup
notes: Technically not a single product — 'Dupont' is a colloquial name for generic 2.54mm crimp housings. Uses female crimp terminals on 0.64mm square pins. Requires crimping tool (Engineer PA-09 or SN-28B). Ubiquitous in maker/hobbyist world. No positive latch — pulls off easily.

name: JST XH (cross-ref Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1A for full details. LiPo balance, LED, Arduino shields.
notes: Cross-reference. Top-entry latch distinguishes from Dupont. PCB header is through-hole vertical or right-angle.

name: JST PH (cross-ref Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1A. LiPo packs, IoT, GPS modules.
notes: Cross-reference. 2mm pitch. De facto LiPo standard.

name: JST SH (cross-ref Phase 1 / Qwiic)
useCases: See Phase 1A / Phase 2A. Qwiic/STEMMA QT I²C.
notes: Cross-reference. 1mm pitch. Qwiic/STEMMA QT standard.

name: JST GH (cross-ref Phase 1 / Pixhawk)
useCases: See Phase 1A. Pixhawk autopilot peripherals.
notes: Cross-reference. 1.25mm pitch. Positive latch is the key differentiator from SH.

name: Molex PicoBlade (53047/53048)
useCases: Laptop internal connections, compact consumer electronics, small motor connections, LED module wiring
notes: Molex's 1.25mm pitch wire-to-board. Competes with JST GH at same pitch but different latch style — NOT interchangeable despite same pitch. Very common inside laptop assemblies.

name: Molex CLIK-Mate (502585/502382)
useCases: Internal sensor harnesses, automotive interior electronics, IoT gateways, compact industrial
notes: Audible and tactile click confirms full mating. Available in 1.5mm and 2.0mm pitch. Designed to replace less secure friction-only connectors in applications needing confirmed connection.

name: Molex Micro-Fit 3.0 (cross-ref Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1A. ATX CPU power, industrial power.
notes: Cross-reference. Dual-row power connector. PCB header available vertical and right-angle.

name: Molex Mini-Fit Jr. (cross-ref Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1A. ATX 24-pin, GPU power.
notes: Cross-reference. Highest-current common PCB power connector.

name: Molex Micro-Fit+ (172965)
useCases: Next-gen industrial power, EV auxiliary boards, BMS module connections, server power distribution boards
notes: Upgraded Micro-Fit with higher current rating (12.5A vs 5A) at same 3mm pitch. Improved terminal design. Drop-in replacement for Micro-Fit 3.0 headers in new designs.

name: TE Connectivity AMP CT (Compact Terminal)
useCases: Automotive ECU wire-to-board, industrial controller PCBs, compact motor drivers, BMS cell tap wiring
locking: Positive latch with TPA (Terminal Position Assurance)
notes: TPA = secondary lock that prevents terminal back-out. Critical for automotive reliability. Available sealed and unsealed. Used on many automotive ECU wire-to-board interfaces.

name: TE Connectivity MTA-100 / MTA-156 (IDC)
useCases: Mass-terminated ribbon cable to PCB, industrial I/O boards, older PLC backplanes, LED sign modules
notes: IDC = press wire into V-slot blade that cuts insulation and makes contact. Fast mass termination without stripping. MTA-100 for signal, MTA-156 for power. Being replaced by discrete crimp connectors.

name: Hirose DF13 (1.25mm Wire-to-Board)
useCases: Older Pixhawk (pre-GH adoption), compact consumer electronics, camera modules, some drone FC peripherals
notes: Was the Pixhawk standard before JST GH adoption. Hirose DF13 and JST GH are both 1.25mm pitch but NOT interchangeable — different housing and terminal geometry.

name: Hirose DF63 (3.96mm Power)
useCases: Power supply output, battery connections, motor power on PCB, UPS internal wiring
notes: Hirose's power connector at same pitch as JST VH (3.96mm) but different mating interface — NOT interchangeable with JST VH.

name: Würth WR-WTB Series
useCases: Industrial PCBs, IoT device harnesses, LED driver boards, general-purpose wire-to-board where reliability over Dupont is needed
notes: Würth's comprehensive wire-to-board family spanning multiple pitches. Good alternative to JST with better datasheet availability and free samples. Similar quality tier.

name: Amass MR30/MR60 (Multi-Rotor Power)
useCases: Drone ESC-to-motor solderless connection, multirotor power distribution boards, 3-phase BLDC connections
notes: 3-pin connector for 3-phase BLDC motors. Replaces soldered motor-to-ESC connections. Keyed to prevent phase swap. MR30 for sub-2000g drones, MR60 for larger.

name: Molex SL 70066/70107 (2.54mm Locking)
useCases: PC front panel headers, fan connectors (3/4-pin), USB internal header, audio front panel header
locking: Friction with polarizing rib + ramp lock
notes: The PC motherboard internal connector — 3-pin fan, 4-pin PWM fan, front panel audio, front panel USB 2.0, power LED, reset switch all use this family or close variants. Polarized via removed pin positions.

name: Hirose DF12 (0.5mm Board-to-Board)
useCases: CPU module to carrier board, embedded SoM (System on Module) connectors, SBC expansion, drone FC stacking
notes: Fine-pitch board-to-board with self-alignment feature. Stacking heights: 3mm to 14mm selectable. Used on many embedded computing modules and carrier boards.

name: Hirose DF40 (0.4mm Board-to-Board)
useCases: Smartphone PCB stacking, wearable device boards, ultra-compact IoT, camera module stacking
notes: 0.4mm pitch — one of the finest pitch board-to-board connectors available. Multiple stacking heights (1.5–4.0mm). Common inside smartphones.

name: Hirose DF17 (0.5mm High-Speed B2B)
useCases: High-speed board interconnects, LVDS display data, PCIe between boards, MIPI signal routing between boards
notes: Shielded variant for high-speed differential signals. Controlled impedance contacts for signal integrity at multi-Gbps rates.

name: Samtec QTH/QSH (High-Speed Tiger Eye)
useCases: FPGA development boards (Xilinx/AMD, Intel/Altera), VITA 57 FMC, high-speed DAQ, radar signal processing boards
notes: Tiger Eye contact system — high reliability beryllium copper. VITA 57 FMC (FPGA Mezzanine Card) standard uses Samtec ASP-134604 (HPC) and ASP-134603 (LPC). Supports 28+ Gbps per lane. Standard on all major FPGA evaluation boards.

name: Samtec QSE/QTE (Tiger Eye Low-Profile)
useCases: Compact embedded stacking, SOM to carrier, industrial controller board stacking
notes: Lower profile than QTH/QSH for space-constrained stacking. Same Tiger Eye contact reliability.

name: Samtec SEARAY (High-Density Array)
useCases: FPGA I/O expansion, high-pin-count processor breakout, ASIC test boards, HPC compute boards
notes: Massive pin count in array format. Up to 800 I/O in compact footprint. Used for high-pin-count FPGA/ASIC breakout. Not to be confused with BGA — these are separable connectors.

name: Samtec MECF/MECA (Micro Edge Card)
useCases: SSD module edge connector, M.2 alternative, compact compute module insertion, embedded storage modules
notes: Micro edge card socket for thin PCB insertion. Alternative to M.2 in custom embedded designs. Samtec provides impedance-matched versions for high-speed signals.

name: Molex SlimStack (0.4/0.5mm)
useCases: Smartphone board stacking, tablet PCB interconnect, laptop daughter-card connections, compact wearables
notes: Molex's fine-pitch B2B family competing with Hirose DF40/DF12. Multiple stacking heights available.

name: Molex Board-to-Board Mezzanine (75/78 series)
useCases: Telecom line cards, network processor boards, server mezzanine cards, high-speed backplane daughter cards
notes: High-pin-count mezzanine for telecom and server applications. Supports 25Gbps+ per differential pair. Compression mount options for backplane applications.

name: TE Connectivity MULTI-BEAM (XLE/HD)
useCases: Power distribution board stacking, server power mid-plane, EV BMS board interconnect, telecom power shelf
notes: High-current board-to-board using beam contacts instead of pins. Single beam carries 100A+. Used for power planes in server/telecom equipment. Eliminates need for cable harness between power boards.

name: Amphenol FCI BergStak (0.4/0.5/0.8mm)
useCases: Consumer electronics PCB stacking, automotive infotainment boards, industrial HMI boards
notes: Amphenol/FCI's comprehensive B2B family. Cross-competes with Hirose DF40/12 and Molex SlimStack at each pitch point.

name: Nvidia Jetson Connector (Samtec 699-pin)
useCases: Jetson Orin NX/Nano module to carrier board, Jetson AGX Orin module connector, embedded AI compute modules
notes: Jetson Orin NX/Nano uses 260-pin SO-DIMM-style edge connector. AGX Orin uses 699-pin Samtec board-to-board. Carrier board design must follow Nvidia's impedance and layout guidelines for high-speed lanes (PCIe Gen 4, USB 3.2, CSI).

name: SO-DIMM 260-pin (DDR4) / 262-pin (DDR5)
useCases: Laptop RAM, embedded SBC RAM (some), Jetson module edge connector format, NUC memory, mini-PC memory
notes: JEDEC-standardized memory module form factor. DDR4 and DDR5 have different key notch positions — cannot be cross-inserted. DDR5 adds on-module voltage regulator (PMIC). Also used as compute module form factor (Jetson, Coral, some Raspberry Pi Compute Modules).

name: DIMM 288-pin (DDR4) / 288-pin (DDR5)
useCases: Desktop RAM, server RAM (RDIMM, LRDIMM), workstation memory
notes: Full-size DIMM. DDR4 and DDR5 have different key positions. Server RDIMM/LRDIMM adds buffering. DDR5 dual-channel per DIMM (2×32-bit vs DDR4 1×64-bit). ECC variants for server/workstation.

name: Raspberry Pi Compute Module Connector (Hirose DF40C-100)
useCases: Raspberry Pi CM4/CM5 module to carrier board, custom RPi carrier designs, industrial RPi deployments
notes: Two Hirose DF40C-100DS connectors per CM4 (200 total contacts). Carries GPIO, PCIe, USB, HDMI, CSI, DSI, Ethernet, power. CM5 uses same connector footprint for backward compatibility.

name: Phoenix PTSA/PTSM (Push-in PCB Terminal)
useCases: Industrial controller PCBs, IoT gateway boards, sensor interface boards, compact PLC designs, LED driver boards
notes: Tool-free wire insertion — push stripped wire in, spring clamp grips. Lever or push-button for release. Faster than screw terminals for production wiring. SMD and THT variants.

name: Wago 2060/2061/2065 PCB SMD (cross-ref Phase 1)
useCases: See Phase 1A. LED modules, compact IoT.
notes: Cross-reference. SMD-mountable push-in for lighting industry.

name: Wago 2604/2606 PCB Terminal Block
useCases: Industrial power supply boards, motor driver PCBs, inverter control boards, power distribution PCBs
notes: Lever-actuated cage clamp on PCB. Same lever mechanism as Wago 221 but in PCB-mount form. High-vibration resistance due to spring contact (no screw loosening).

name: Würth Elektronik WR-TBL Series
useCases: General-purpose PCB screw terminals, power supply boards, motor controllers, charging stations, Indian industrial PCBs
notes: Würth's comprehensive PCB terminal block range. Covers every standard pitch. Screw, rising cage, and spring cage variants. Rising cage is preferred over standard screw for stranded wire (won't splay strands). Free samples from Würth.

name: TE Connectivity Buchanan PCB Terminal Block
useCases: Legacy industrial PCBs, existing designs using TE terminals, power supply output stages
notes: TE's legacy PCB terminal block line. Still available but new designs typically choose Phoenix COMBICON, Wago, or Würth for better availability and documentation.

name: Keystone / Eurostyle PCB Screw Terminal (generic)
useCases: Budget consumer electronics PCBs, LED controllers, cheap motor drivers, home automation modules, student projects
notes: Ultra-low-cost generic PCB screw terminals. Often unbranded from Shenzhen. Adequate for low-vibration consumer applications. Blue or green housing typical. Available on AliExpress/LCSC in bulk.

name: Screwless/Lever PCB Terminal (various)
useCases: Modern industrial PCB designs, IoT devices, compact controllers, where tool-free wiring is specified
notes: Industry trend: screwless terminals replacing screw terminals on new industrial PCB designs. No screw loosening from vibration. Faster production wiring. Push-in or lever-operated variants.

name: IDC Ribbon Cable Connector (2x5, 2x7, 2x10, 2x17, 2x20, 2x25)
useCases: JTAG/SWD debug (2x5 10-pin), Cortex Debug (2x10 20-pin), ISP programming, parallel GPIO buses, old IDE (2x20 40-pin)
legacy: Declining for data, alive for debug
notes: Insulation displacement onto flat ribbon cable. Red stripe = pin 1. Polarizing notch on header prevents reverse insertion. 10-pin (2×5) is standard for ARM SWD/JTAG debug (Segger J-Link, ST-Link). 20-pin ARM standard debug. 40-pin IDE is legacy.

name: Pogo Pin / Spring-Loaded Contact (PCB-mount)
useCases: PCB test jigs (bed-of-nails), programming fixtures, battery contacts (phones, wearables), magnetic charging docks, CubeSat deployment switches
notes: Spring-loaded pins for temporary or repeated contact. Bed-of-nails test fixtures use hundreds of pogo pins to test assembled PCBs. Also used as battery contacts in consumer devices. Mill-Max is the premium supplier.

name: PCIe CEM (cross-ref Phase 3)
useCases: See Phase 3C. GPU, NVMe, FPGA, AI accelerator.
notes: Cross-reference.

name: M.2 (cross-ref Phase 3)
useCases: See Phase 3C. NVMe SSD, WiFi, cellular modem.
notes: Cross-reference.

name: CompactPCI (cPCI) — 2mm Hard Metric
useCases: Telecom switches, railway control, military ground stations, industrial computing, traffic management, ISRO ground segment
notes: Based on Eurocard 3U/6U form factor. Hot-swap capable. Still deployed in Indian Railways signaling, ISRO ground stations, and telecom central offices. Being replaced by CompactPCI Serial (PCIe-based).

name: CompactPCI Serial (CPCI-S)
useCases: Modern telecom, railway signaling (ETCS/Kavach), defense ground stations, traffic management, rugged computing
notes: PCIe-based successor to parallel cPCI. Supports PCIe Gen 3, USB 3.0, SATA, Ethernet on backplane. Same 3U/6U Eurocard form factor for rack compatibility.

name: VMEbus (DIN 41612)
useCases: Military computing (legacy), physics experiments (CERN NIM/CAMAC replacement), older industrial control, nuclear I&C
notes: VMEbus was THE military/aerospace embedded computing standard for decades. DIN 41612 connector is the iconic 3-row 96-pin backplane connector. Still in service on many fielded military systems. Being replaced by VPX.

name: VPX / OpenVPX (VITA 46/48)
useCases: Modern military embedded computing, radar processing, electronic warfare, UAV compute payloads, shipboard combat systems, Indian Navy CMS
notes: Successor to VME for military. High-speed serial backplane (PCIe Gen 3, 10GbE, Serial RapidIO, InfiniBand). Conduction-cooled variants (VITA 48) for sealed enclosures — no fans/air needed. OpenVPX (VITA 65) standardizes slot profiles. Used on Indian Navy Shivalik-class CMS and DRDO radar systems.

name: VME64x / VXS (VITA 41)
useCases: Transitional systems between VME and VPX, some radar, sonar, telecom still using VME64x infrastructure
notes: VME64x added power connectors and improved pin count. VXS added high-speed serial fabric to VME backplane. Bridging generation between VME and VPX.

name: VITA 57 FMC/FMC+ (Samtec-based, cross-ref 6B)
useCases: FPGA daughter cards for ADC/DAC, RF front-ends, high-speed I/O, custom interfaces on Xilinx/Intel FPGA eval boards
notes: FPGA Mezzanine Card standard. LPC (Low Pin Count) for simpler I/O, HPC (High Pin Count) for multi-gigabit transceivers, FMC+ extends to 28Gbps+. Samtec Searay/QTH connectors. Standard on all major FPGA eval boards (Xilinx ZCU/Versal, Intel Agilex).

name: PXI / PXIe (CompactPCI-based)
useCases: Automated test equipment (ATE), modular instruments, semiconductor test, production test, military ATE
notes: NI (now part of Emerson) PXI platform for modular test instruments. PXI = CompactPCI + timing/triggering. PXIe = CompactPCI Serial + timing/triggering. 3U form factor. Hundreds of instrument modules available: oscilloscopes, DMMs, signal generators, RF analyzers.

name: PICMG 1.3 (SHB/SBC Backplane)
useCases: Industrial PC backplanes, rackmount IPCs, machine vision PCs, SCADA workstations
notes: System Host Board standard — CPU card plugs into passive backplane. Allows CPU upgrade without changing I/O cards. Being replaced by COM Express modular SBCs in new designs.

name: COM Express (Samtec/TE connectors)
useCases: Ruggedized industrial SBCs, medical imaging PCs, military mission computers, vision processing, autonomous vehicle compute
notes: Computer-on-Module standard. CPU module with standard connector plugs into custom carrier board. Carrier has application-specific I/O, module has compute. Types: Mini (84×55mm), Compact (95×95mm), Basic (125×95mm). Allows CPU upgrade without carrier redesign. Intel and AMD processors.

name: SMARC (Smart Mobility Architecture)
useCases: ARM-based embedded systems, IoT gateways, edge AI compute, digital signage, vending machines, drones, robots
notes: ARM-focused SoM standard (though x86 also possible). Uses MXM 3.0 connector (originally designed for mobile GPU modules). Smaller than COM Express. Popular for NXP i.MX8, Qualcomm, Rockchip, Nvidia Jetson alternative platforms.

name: Qseven (Q7)
useCases: Ultra-compact embedded SBCs, handheld devices, portable instruments, compact robotics controllers
notes: Smaller than SMARC (70×70mm). Same MXM connector family. Designed for ultra-compact, low-power embedded. x86 and ARM options.

name: SIM Card Socket (Nano/Micro/Mini)
useCases: Cellular modems, IoT devices with LTE/5G, GPS trackers, SBCs with cellular (Sixfab, Waveshare), M2M gateways
notes: Nano-SIM (4FF) is current standard. eSIM (embedded) is replacing physical SIM in some devices. Push-push mechanism: push to insert, push again to eject. Some use tray (iPhone-style, board-soldered).

name: SD/MicroSD Card Socket
useCases: Raspberry Pi, cameras, data loggers, audio recorders, 3D printers (Marlin), embedded Linux boot media
locking: Push-push spring eject or friction hold
notes: MicroSD is dominant. SD UHS-I: 104MB/s, UHS-II: 312MB/s (extra row of pins). SDIO variant carries peripheral I/O (WiFi, BT) instead of storage. RPi boot from MicroSD is the most common use case in embedded.

name: USB Type-A PCB Socket (horizontal/vertical)
useCases: USB host port on PCBs, Raspberry Pi USB, industrial PC USB, embedded device USB host
notes: Through-hole or SMD PCB mount. Shield tabs provide mechanical retention and grounding. USB 3.x socket (blue) has additional pin row at back.

name: USB Type-C PCB Receptacle
useCases: Embedded device USB-C port, Raspberry Pi 5 power input, Jetson carrier USB-C, modern dev board programming/power port
notes: Mid-mount and top-mount PCB variants. CC pins require 5.1kΩ pulldown for basic sink (power consumer). For USB PD, dedicated PD controller IC required. 16-pin simplified variant available for USB 2.0-only applications.

name: RJ45 PCB Jack (with integrated magnetics)
useCases: Ethernet port on PCBs, Raspberry Pi Ethernet, switch boards, industrial controller Ethernet, IoT gateways
notes: Integrated magnetics (Bob Smith termination inside) simplify PCB design — eliminates external transformer. Tab-up and tab-down variants for different PCB orientations. LED indicators (green=link, amber=activity) integrated. PoE-rated jacks have reinforced contacts.

name: DC Barrel Jack PCB Mount
useCases: Arduino power input, router boards, small embedded PCBs, lab power supplies, 3D printer controller boards
notes: PCB-mount barrel jack. Third pin is normally-closed switch that opens when plug is inserted — can be used for automatic battery/external power switching. 2.1mm and 2.5mm center pin variants.

name: Coin Cell Battery Holder (CR2032/CR2025)
useCases: RTC backup battery, BIOS/CMOS battery, IoT sleep-mode power, BLE beacons, small sensor nodes
notes: Through-hole or SMD variants. Horizontal and vertical mounting. Critical detail: some holders have polarity protection, some don't — reverse insertion shorts the cell. CR2032 is the dominant size.

name: Battery Holder (AA/AAA/18650)
useCases: Handheld devices, remote controls, sensor nodes, IoT field devices, 18650 battery packs, LED flashlight PCBs, battery-powered robots
notes: PCB-mount or chassis-mount. 18650 holders critical for DIY battery packs and BMS projects. Keystone 1042/1043 series is industry standard for 18650.

name: Blade Fuse PCB Holder (ATO/Mini)
useCases: Automotive PCBs, power distribution boards, BMS boards, ESC boards, drone power boards
notes: PCB-mount holder accepts standard automotive blade fuses. Through-hole or surface-mount. Allows field-replaceable fusing on PCBs without soldering.

name: BT2.0
useCases: 1S brushless tiny whoops (Meteor65/75, Air65, Cetus), 1S FC pigtails, retrofit of PH2.0 whoops
notes: BetaFPV proprietary, introduced 2019 to fix PH2.0 voltage sag. 1.0mm solid banana contacts instead of crimped pins. Male 6.0x4.2x10.5mm / 0.34g; female 6.0x4.2x8.3mm / 0.24g. Pigtail: blue = positive, white = negative. Independent testing at 12A found BT2.0, A30, GNB27 and XT30 within noise of each other; only PH2.0 underperformed.
